originalTitle: Zambia ex-president's family wins latest legal battle over what should happen to his body
title: Happy News for a Family in Zambia!
content:
Hello, my super friends across Africa! I'm your kid-friendly news reporter, and I have a special story from our wonderful country, Zambia. It’s a story about a very important person, his family, and how grown-ups solve big problems.

Who Was President Edgar Lungu?

Let's talk about a kind man named Mr. Edgar Lungu. He was once the *president* of Zambia! Do you know what a president does? A president is like the head teacher of a whole country. They help make sure everyone is safe, happy, and that schools, roads, and hospitals are good. Mr. Lungu was the leader of Zambia for some time, making important decisions for all the people living there. Did you know? Zambia is a beautiful country in Southern Africa, famous for its amazing wildlife and the mighty Victoria Falls!

A Sad Time and a Big Question

Sadly, a little while ago, Mr. Lungu passed away. This means he went to a peaceful rest. When someone important like a former president passes away, their family and the whole country feel very sad. After someone passes away, families usually decide where they want their loved one to be buried. This is a special place where they can rest, and family and friends can visit to remember them. But for Mr. Lungu, there was a big question. His family and the government (that's like the team of people who help the president run the country now) had different ideas about where he should be buried. This argument went on for a whole year! Imagine arguing about something for a whole year. It can be very tiring for everyone involved.

What is a Family's Wish?

Families are very special. They love each other and want the best for their members, even after they have passed away. Mr. Lungu's family had a very clear idea of where they wanted him to rest. They believed it was important to honor his memory in a way that felt right to them. In many African cultures, respecting the wishes of the family for burial is very important. It's how we show love and honor. Did you know? Families often choose a special place, like a family burial ground or a quiet spot, because it holds many memories for them.

What is a Government?

The government is a team of grown-ups who work together to manage the country. They help with many things like keeping our streets safe, making sure we have clean water, and building schools. Sometimes, when a very important person like a former president passes away, the government might also want to be involved because the person was important to the whole nation. They might want a special place to honor them for everyone to remember. Did you know? The capital city of Zambia is Lusaka, a very busy and exciting city!

Going to Court: A Way to Solve Problems Peacefully

When grown-ups can't agree on something important, they sometimes go to a *court*. A court is a special place where a wise person called a *judge* listens to everyone's side of the story. The judge's job is to listen carefully and then make a fair decision based on the rules and what is right. It's like when you and your friend can't decide which game to play, and a grown-up helps you decide fairly. So, Mr. Lungu's family went to court. They asked the judge to help them decide what should happen to Mr. Lungu's resting place. This is called a 'legal battle' – it sounds like a fight, but it's really just a way to solve disagreements using rules and talking, not actual fighting.

Good News! The Family Won!

And guess what? After a whole year of waiting and talking, the wonderful news came! The judge listened to everyone and decided that the family's wishes should be followed. This means the family now has the right to decide where Mr. Lungu will finally rest peacefully. It was a big win for them! They were very happy because their voices were heard, and their wishes were respected. This decision means that the long argument is now over. The family can finally have peace and give Mr. Lungu the respectful resting place they have wanted for him. Did you know? Solving problems peacefully, like in a court, is very important for a country to be fair and happy.

Why is this Important?

This news is important for many reasons. First, it shows that even presidents and their families have rights, and their feelings matter. Second, it teaches us that when people disagree, going to court and letting a judge decide is a good, peaceful way to find a solution. It helps everyone understand what is fair. Most importantly, it means that Mr. Lungu's family can now move forward with their plans and finally lay him to rest. It brings comfort to them to know that their loved one will be honored in the way they wished.

A Time for Peace and Remembering

Now, everyone in Zambia, especially Mr. Lungu's family, can look forward to a time of peace. They can now remember him with love and respect, without the sadness of the argument hanging over them. This story reminds us all about the importance of family, respect, and finding peaceful ways to solve big problems. So, from your kid-friendly reporter, that’s the happy news from Zambia! We celebrate with Mr. Lungu's family and hope that he rests in peace.
summary: After a year-long disagreement, former Zambian president Edgar Lungu's family won a legal battle, meaning they can now decide where he will finally be buried, bringing them peace.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that families have the right to decide how to honor their loved ones, and going to court is a peaceful way for grown-ups to solve big disagreements fairly.

title: Niger pulls out of International Criminal Court after calling it neo-colonialist
content: Niger, along with Mali and Burkina Faso, had called the ICC an "instrument of neo-colonialist repression".

originalTitle: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
title: Fun Football News: What We Learned from England and Ghana!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here, ready to share some exciting (and sometimes not-so-exciting) news from the world of sports! Today, we are talking about a big football game. Football is so much fun, isn't it? It makes us cheer and sometimes even jump with joy! Today's story is about two big football teams: England and Ghana.

What Happened in the Big Game?

So, there was a football match between the team from England and our friends from Ghana. Ghana is a wonderful country right here in Africa! Everyone was excited to watch them play. But guess what? In this game, something special happened. Both teams played very, very hard. They kicked the ball, they ran fast, and they tried their best to score goals. However, by the time the game was over, no one had scored any goals! Can you believe it? The score was zero to zero. We call this a 'goalless draw' or a 'tie'. Did you know? A 'draw' in football means both teams finish with the same number of goals. If both have zero, it's a goalless draw!

No Need to Be Worried!

When a game ends with no goals, some people might feel a little sad or think it was a boring game. But our news today says: 'No need to panic!' What does 'no need to panic' mean? It means there is no reason to be scared or worried. It's just one game. Sometimes, even the best teams have days where they don't score. It's like when you play a game with your friends, and sometimes you win, and sometimes you don't. It's all part of playing and having fun. Losing or drawing doesn't mean you're not good players! Every team, even big ones like England and Ghana, has good days and not-so-good days. And that's totally okay! What matters is that they tried their best.

What is a 'Reality Check'?

The news also said this game was a 'reality check' for England. That sounds like a big grown-up phrase, doesn't it? Let's make it simple! A 'reality check' is like a moment when you learn something important. It's when you think you know everything, but then something happens that shows you might need to try harder or learn more. Imagine you are playing hide-and-seek, and you think you are the best at hiding. But then your friend finds you super fast! That's a 'reality check'. It shows you might need new hiding spots! For the England football team, this game showed them that they are good, but they still have things to learn and practice. They learned that even against a strong team like Ghana, they need to be even better. It helps them to know what they need to work on. Maybe they need to practice their shooting more. Or maybe they need to work together even better as a team. It's a chance to grow stronger!

Who are England and Ghana?

England is a big country far away, and they have a very famous football team. They play in many big competitions around the world. And Ghana? Ghana is one of Africa's amazing countries! They also have a super talented football team, often called the 'Black Stars'. We are always so proud to see African teams play on the world stage! Did you know? Ghana's football team has played in the FIFA World Cup, which is a huge football tournament where teams from all over the world play! This game was a friendly game, not a super important one for a big cup. Friendly games are great because teams can try new things and learn from each other without too much pressure.

Learning and Growing in Football (and Life!)

Football is not just about winning or losing. It's also about learning. Every game is a chance to learn something new about yourself and your team. When England had a 'reality check', it means they looked at their game and thought, "Hmm, we can do better!" And that's a wonderful thing. It helps them to become even stronger for next time. It's just like when you are learning something new in school. Maybe you don't get it right the first time. But you don't give up! You try again, and you learn from your mistakes. Did you know? Football players spend many hours practicing every single day to get better at kicking, passing, and scoring goals!

Teamwork Makes the Dream Work!

In football, teamwork is super important. Even if one player is very, very good, the whole team needs to work together. They need to pass the ball, help each other, and cheer each other on. This game reminded both England and Ghana that working together makes them stronger. It's like in your class: when everyone helps each other, everyone learns more! So, even though there were no goals, this game taught everyone a big lesson about trying hard, learning from challenges, and never giving up. It's what makes football the most beautiful game in the world!

Keep Cheering!

So, remember, kids: no need to panic! Football is fun, and every game, win or draw, teaches us something important. Keep cheering for your favourite teams, especially our amazing African teams! They always make us proud. That's all for today from your kid-friendly news reporter. See you next time for more exciting news!
summary: A football match between England and Ghana ended with no goals, but this 'goalless draw' taught England an important lesson to work harder, reminding everyone that learning and teamwork are key in sports.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows that even big teams learn from every game, and it's okay not to win all the time. It teaches us about being a good sport, working together, and always trying your best, which are great lessons for football and for life!
